item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.
notes: Agent A starts pro-society but with explicit rights caveats, softens toward balance at turn 3, then returns to supporting societal priority at turn 5 while retaining rights and autonomy safeguards. Agent B consistently argues that societal interests should not override fundamental freedoms, and becomes slightly firmer by turn 6 around national-security overreach and state accountability. No complete flip is visible.
